phases.title: Rust backend: rate-limit types, `fetch_rate_limit_status` Tauri command, central `gh_error_message` helper
verification: `github.rs` defines `RateLimitBucket { limit, used, remaining, reset }` with `#[serde(rename_all="camelCase")]` and `RateLimitStatus { core, search, graphql, fetched_at_epoch: u64 }` (Option B chosen â€” no chrono dependency).

`fetch_rate_limit_status(project_path: Option<String>) -> Result<RateLimitStatus, String>` is `#[tauri::command]`, calls `gh api rate_limit`, parses the `resources` wrapper into the three exposed buckets, and stamps `fetched_at_epoch` from `SystemTime::now()`.
On `gh api` failure, the function calls `gh_error_message(&stderr)` rather than reproducing inline error matching.
`classify_gh_error(stderr) -> GhErrorKind` discriminates `SecondaryRateLimit` BEFORE `RateLimitExceeded` (substring overlap correctness).
`gh_error_message(stderr) -> String` returns user-facing strings for all five `GhErrorKind` variants â€” rate-limit messages reference the footer indicator and authentication hint.
`lib.rs` `invoke_handler` lists `github::fetch_rate_limit_status` exactly once.
No new Rust dependencies added to `Cargo.toml` (std + serde + serde_json only).
Existing 28 Rust tests pass; `cargo check` from `apps/desktop/src-tauri` is clean (no new warnings introduced by this change).
phases.summary: Added GhErrorKind enum + classify_gh_error/gh_error_message helpers (centralizes the duplicated stderr matching + adds rate-limit detection), RateLimitBucket/RateLimitStatus types (camelCase serde, fetched_at_epoch as u64 to avoid chrono dep), GhRateLimitResponse internal wrapper, and fetch_rate_limit_status Tauri command (calls 'gh api rate_limit', parses resources.{core,search,graphql}). Registered command in lib.rs invoke_handler. cargo check clean in 28.66s, no new warnings.

phases.title: Synchronous retry wrapper for `gh` read commands + refactor read commands to use centralized error handling
verification: `github.rs` defines `run_gh_with_retry<F>(factory: F) -> Result<Output, String> where F: FnMut() -> Command` with a `GH_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECS: &[u64] = &[2, 4, 8]` constant.
Wrapper retries ONLY on `GhErrorKind::SecondaryRateLimit`; all other errors return immediately via `gh_error_message`.
Wrapper uses `std::thread::sleep` (not async) and logs each retry via `log::warn!` with attempt number and delay.

All 7 read commands (`fetch_github_prs`, `fetch_github_pr_detail`, `fetch_github_issues`, `fetch_github_issue_by_number`, `fetch_github_releases`, `fetch_github_labels`, `fetch_issue_comments`) route their gh invocation through `run_gh_with_retry`.

Each refactored read command no longer contains its own `stderr.contains("not logged in")` / `stderr.contains("not a git repository")` blocks â€” they're replaced by `gh_error_message` (called inside the wrapper on the final attempt).
Write commands (`create_github_issue`, `edit_github_issue`, `close_github_issue`, `post_issue_comment`), git commands, Claude CLI commands, and `check_*` status commands remain unchanged.
`fetch_rate_limit_status` (Phase 1) does NOT use the retry wrapper.
`cargo check` clean (no new warnings); `cargo test --lib` preserves the 28-test baseline.
phases.summary: Added run_gh_with_retry<F: FnMut() -> Command> helper with const GH_RETRY_BACKOFF_SECS = [2, 4, 8] (4 attempts total, 14s worst-case wait). Retries ONLY on GhErrorKind::SecondaryRateLimit, returns immediately for all other classifications via gh_error_message. Refactored 7 read commands to route through the wrapper: fetch_github_prs, fetch_github_pr_detail, fetch_github_issues, fetch_github_issue_by_number, fetch_github_releases, fetch_github_labels, fetch_issue_comments â€” each now uses move closure with let-Some-ref project_path borrow inside. Eliminated ~40 lines of duplicated stderr-pattern matching. Write commands (create/edit/close/comment), git/claude CLI helpers, and check_* status commands intentionally unchanged. cargo check clean in 3.9s, no new warnings.

phases.title: Frontend: `<RateLimitIndicator>` polled component, footer mount, IPC binding, vitest coverage
verification: `apps/desktop/src/components/RateLimitIndicator.tsx` exports a `RateLimitIndicator` component that calls `invoke<RateLimitStatus>('fetch_rate_limit_status', { projectPath: null })` after a 1.5s initial delay and every 60s thereafter, cleaning up both timers on unmount.

Component renders `GH: <remaining>/<limit>` of the `core` bucket with severity class `--healthy` (â‰Ą50%), `--warn` (10â€“50%), or `--critical` (<10%); shows `GH: --/--` when no data; `GH: !` with title=error when fetch fails.
Click or hover toggles a tooltip listing Core / Search / GraphQL each with `remaining/limit Â· resets in Xm/h Ym`.
`Header.tsx` mounts `<RateLimitIndicator />` in a new `.footer-right` flex group together with the existing Reset Layout button; path stays leftmost.
`App.css` gains `.footer-right { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 10px }`; `.footer`'s existing `space-between` is preserved.
`__tests__/RateLimitIndicator.test.tsx` adds 3 vitest cases (healthy render, critical render, tooltip toggle) with `@tauri-apps/api/core` mocked via `vi.mock`.
No new package dependencies; no new Zustand stores; existing footer behavior (path display, Reset Layout) unchanged.
`pnpm build` clean (tsc -b + vite); `pnpm test` desktop suite goes from 89 â†’ 92 (3 new tests); 28 Rust tests still pass.
phases.summary: Created RateLimitIndicator.tsx + RateLimitIndicator.css + __tests__/RateLimitIndicator.test.tsx (3 vitest cases). Modified Header.tsx (footer-right flex group wraps RateLimitIndicator + Reset Layout button) and App.css (.footer-right rule). pnpm build clean (only pre-existing 1.5MB chunk-size warning); vitest counts shared 65/65, desktop 89â†’92 (+3 new), Rust 28/28. Two deviations from plan: (1) tests are helper-based (severityFor / formatReset / data-shape assertions) not DOM-render â€” project's vitest is configured with environment:'node' and has no jsdom or @testing-library/react dep. (2) The Phase 3 sub-agent attempted to add jsdom + testing-library but hit the Windows reparse-point block (CLAUDE.md gotcha) mid-install, then manually copied vitest into apps/desktop/node_modules/vitest as a real directory to recover. Next elevated `pnpm install` will heal that to a junction. Hex color fallbacks in CSS (var(--success-color, #4ade80) etc.) were necessary â€” semantic color tokens don't exist in the project's CSS variables. Component fully implements the planned UI: severity-colored status, click/hover tooltip with all 3 buckets + reset times, 1.5s initial fetch + 60s polling, error and loading states.
